sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: νύμφαι πετραῖαι , πολλάκις με βουθυτεῖν
sections.translation: Nymphs of the rocks , may I and my wife , the daughter of Tyndareus , often sacrifice at home , in good fortune as now , and may my enemies suffer — meaning you and Orestes . But my master prayed for the opposite , not speaking the words aloud ,

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: λαβεῖν πατρῷα δώματʼ . ἐκ κανοῦ δʼ ἑλὼν
sections.translation: that he might win his father ’ s house . Aegisthus took from a basket a long straight knife , and cutting off some of the calf ’ s hair laid it with his right hand on the sacred fire , and then cut the calf ’ s throat when the servants had lifted it upon their shoulders , and said this to your brother :

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: Ἐκ τῶν καλῶν κομποῦσι τοῖσι Θεσσαλοῖς
sections.translation: They boast that this is among the honorable accomplishments for the Thessalians : to cut up a bull rightly and to tame horses ; take the knife , stranger , and show us if the report about the Thessalians is true .

sections.label: Section 819
sections.original: ὁ δʼ εὐκρότητον Δωρίδʼ ἁρπάσας χεροῖν ,
sections.translation: Orestes seized in his hands the well-hammered Dorian knife and

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: ῥίψας ἀπʼ ὤμων εὐπρεπῆ πορπάματα ,
sections.translation: threw from his shoulders his graceful buckled robe ; he chose Pylades as an assistant in the work and drove back the servants ; and taking the calf by the hoof , he laid bare its white flesh , with arm outstretched , and flayed the hide quicker than a runner

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: δισσοὺς διαύλους ἱππίους διήνυσε ,
sections.translation: finishes the two laps of the horses ’ race-course ; and then he laid the flanks open . Aegisthus took the entrails in his hands and inspected them . Now the liver had no lobe , while the portal vein and near-by gall-bladder revealed threatening approaches to the one who was observing it .

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: χὣ μὲν σκυθράζει , δεσπότης δʼ ἀνιστορεῖ ·
sections.translation: Aegisthus was angry , but my master asked , Why are you disheartened ? Stranger , I fear some treachery from abroad . Agamemnon ’ s son is the man I hate most , and an enemy to my house . But Orestes said , Do you really fear treachery from an exile ,

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: πόλεως ἀνάσσων ; οὐχ , ὅπως παστήρια
sections.translation: when you rule the city ? Instead of the Dorian knife , let someone bring me a Thessalian axe and let me split the breast-bone , so that we may hold the sacrificial feast . He took the axe and cut . Now Aegisthus took up the entrails , and was inspecting and sorting them out . As he was bending down ,

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: ὄνυχας ἐπʼ ἄκρους στὰς κασίγνητος σέθεν
sections.translation: your brother rose on tiptoe and struck him on the spine ; his back-bone broke apart ; with his whole body he struggled up and down , and cried out , dying hard in his blood . As soon as the servants saw it , they rushed to arms ,

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: πολλοὶ μάχεσθαι πρὸς δύʼ · ἀνδρείας δʼ ὕπο
sections.translation: many to fight against two ; yet Pylades and Orestes in their bravery stood to face them , brandishing their weapons . Then he said : I do not come hostile to this city or to my own servants ; I , the unhappy Orestes , have avenged myself on the murderer of my father ;

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: τλήμων Ὀρέστης · ἀλλὰ μή με καίνετε ,
sections.translation: but do not kill me , old servants of my father ! They , when they heard his words , held back their spears , and he was recognized by an old man , who had been long in the household . Immediately they crowned your brother with a wreath , and shouted with joy .

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: χαίροντες ἀλαλάζοντες . ἔρχεται δὲ σοὶ
sections.translation: And he comes bringing a head to show you , not that of the Gorgon , but of the one you hate , Aegisthus ; his death today has paid in blood a bitter debt of blood .

sections.label: Section 859
sections.original: θὲς ἐς χορόν , ὦ φίλα , ἴχνος ,
sections.translation: Set your step to the dance , my dear ,

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: ὡς νεβρὸς οὐράνιον
sections.translation: like a fawn leaping high up to heaven with joy . Your brother is victorious and has accomplished the wearing of a crown . . . beside the streams of Alpheus . Come sing

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: καλλίνικον ᾠδὰν ἐμῷ χορῷ .
sections.translation: a glorious victory ode , to my dance .

sections.label: Section 866
sections.original: ὦ φέγγος , ὦ τέθριππον ἡλίου σέλας ,
sections.translation: O light , O blaze of the sun , drawn by its team ! O earth and night , all that I saw before ; now I am free to open my eyes , for Aegisthus , my father ’ s murderer , has fallen .

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: φέρʼ , οἷα δὴ χω καὶ δόμοι κεύθουσί μου
sections.translation: Come , let me bring out whatever adornment for hair that I have and my house contains , friends , and I shall wreath the head of my conquering brother .

sections.label: Section 873
sections.original: σὺ μέν νυν ἀγάλματʼ ἄειρε
sections.translation: It is for you to bring adornment now for his head ;

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: χωρήσεται Μούσαισι χόρευμα φίλον .
sections.translation: our dance , dear to the Muses , will go on . Now , those who were once our dear kings will rule our land justly , having destroyed the unjust . So let the shout , harmonious with joy , go up .

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: ὦ καλλίνικε , πατρὸς ἐκ νικηφόρου
sections.translation: O glorious victor , Orestes , son of a father victorious in battle under Troy , receive this band for the locks of your hair . You have come home , running a contest of the stadium that was not useless , but rather killing

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: Αἴγισθον , ὃς σὸν πατέρα κἀμὸν ὤλεσε .
sections.translation: Aegisthus , the murderer of your father and mine . And you , his companion , Pylades , taught by a most pious father , receive a garland from my hand ; for you also bear an equal part of the contest , with Orestes . May you always seem to me fortunate !

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: θεοὺς μὲν ἡγοῦ πρῶτον , Ἠλέκτρα , τύχης
sections.translation: First believe that the gods , Electra , are the leaders of our fortune , and then praise me as the servant of them and of fate . I come , having killed Aegisthus not in word but in deed ; to add this proof to your knowledge ,

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: προσθῶμεν , αὐτὸν τὸν θανόντα σοι φέρω ,
sections.translation: I am bringing you his corpse , which , if you wish , you may expose as prey for wild animals or impale and press it down on a stake as spoil for birds , the children of the air ; for now he is your slave , once called your master .

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: αἰσχύνομαι μέν , βούλομαι δʼ εἰπεῖν ὅμως .
sections.translation: I am ashamed , but equally I wish to speak .

sections.label: Section 901
sections.original: τί χρῆμα ; λέξον · ὡς φόβου γʼ ἔξωθεν εἶ .
sections.translation: What is it ? Speak , as you are free from fear .

sections.label: Section 902
sections.original: νεκροὺς ὑβρίζειν , μή μέ τις φθόνῳ βάλῃ .
sections.translation: I am ashamed to insult the dead , for fear someone might hurl malice at me .

sections.label: Section 903
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν οὐδεὶς ὅστις ἂν μέμψαιτό σε .
sections.translation: There is no one who would blame you .

sections.label: Section 904
sections.original: δυσάρεστος ἡμῶν καὶ φιλόψογος πόλις .
sections.translation: Our citizens are hard to please , and love scandal .

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: λέγʼ , εἴ τι χρῄζεις , σύγγονʼ · ἀσπόνδοισι γὰρ
sections.translation: Speak , if you need to say anything , sister ; for we engaged in hostilities with him on terms without truce .

sections.label: Section 907
sections.original: εἶἑν · τίνʼ ἀρχὴν πρῶτά σʼ ἐξείπω κακῶν ,
sections.translation: Well then ! Which of your evil acts shall I tell of first , as a beginning ? What sort of end shall I make ? What part of my speech shall I assign to the middle place ? And yet I never ceased , throughout the early mornings ,

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: θρυλοῦσʼ ἅ γʼ εἰπεῖν ἤθελον κατʼ ὄμμα σόν ,
sections.translation: repeating what I wished to say to your face , if ever I were free from my old terrors . And now I am ; so I will pay you back with those reproaches I wanted to make when you were alive . You destroyed me , and orphaned me

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: καὶ τόνδʼ ἔθηκας , οὐδὲν ἠδικημένος ,
sections.translation: and this man here of a dear father , though you were wronged in no way by us ; and you made a shameful marriage with my mother , and killed her husband , who led the armies of Hellas , though you never went to Troy . You were so foolish that you really expected , in marrying my mother , that she would not be unfaithful to you ,

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: γήμας , ἐμοῦ δὲ πατρὸς ἠδίκεις λέχη .
sections.translation: though you were wronging my father ’ s bed . Know that whoever ruins another ’ s wife , in secret love , and then is forced to take her himself , is pitiable , if he thinks that the chastity which did not govern her before will do so with him .

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: ἄλγιστα δʼ ᾤκεις , οὐ δοκῶν οἰκεῖν κακῶς ·
sections.translation: You lived most miserably , although you thought it otherwise ; you knew well that you had made an unholy marriage , and my mother knew that she had in you an impious husband . Both being wicked , she took up your fortune , you her evil .

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: πᾶσιν δʼ ἐν Ἀργείοισιν ἤκουες τάδε ·
sections.translation: Among all the Argives you would hear this : That woman ’ s husband , not that man ’ s wife . Although this is a shameful thing , for the wife to rule the house and not the husband ; and I hate those children who are called in the city not the sons of the man , their father ,

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: οὐκ ὠνόμασται , τῆς δὲ μητρὸς ἐν πόλει .
sections.translation: but of their mother . For instance , when a man makes a remarkable marriage , one above his rank , there is no talk of the husband but only of the wife . This deceived you the most , in your ignorance : you professed to be some one , strong in your wealth ,

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: τὰ δʼ οὐδὲν εἰ μὴ βραχὺν ὁμιλῆσαι χρόνον .
sections.translation: but that is nothing , except to associate with briefly . It is nature that is secure , not wealth ; for , always standing by , it takes away troubles ; but prosperity , when it lives wickedly and with fools , flies out of the house , flowering for a short time .

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: ἃ δʼ ἐς γυναῖκας — παρθένῳ γὰρ οὐ καλὸν
sections.translation: As to your women , I am silent — for it is not good for a maiden to speak of this — but I will tell riddles that can be understood . You were insolent because you had a king ’ s house and were endowed with good looks . May I never have a husband with a girl ’ s face , but one with a man ’ s ways .

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: τὰ γὰρ τέκνʼ αὐτῶν Ἄρεος ἐκκρεμάννυται ,
sections.translation: For the children of the latter cling to a life of arms , while the fair ones are only an ornament in the dance . Spurning the corpse with her foot Begone , knowing nothing of how you were discovered and paid the penalty in time . So let no evildoer suppose , even if he runs the first step well ,

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: νικᾶν δοκείτω τὴν Δίκην , πρὶν ἂν πέλας
sections.translation: that he will get the better of Justice , until he comes to the end of the finish-line and makes the last turn in life .

sections.label: Section 957
sections.original: ἔπραξε δεινά , δεινὰ δʼ ἀντέδωκε σοὶ
sections.translation: He did terrible things , and repaid them to you and Orestes ; for Justice has great strength .

sections.label: Section 959
sections.original: εἶἑν · κομίζειν τοῦδε σῶμʼ ἔσω χρεὼν
sections.translation: Well then ; you must carry the body of this man inside

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: σκότῳ τε δοῦναι , δμῶες , ὡς , ὅταν μόλῃ
sections.translation: and hide it , slaves , so that when my mother comes , she may not see his corpse before her slaughter .

sections.label: Section 962
sections.original: ἐπίσχες · ἐμβάλωμεν εἰς ἄλλον λόγον .
sections.translation: Wait ! Let us go into another matter .

sections.label: Section 963
sections.original: τί δʼ ; ἐκ Μυκηνῶν μῶν βοηδρόμους ὁρῶ ;
sections.translation: What ? Those are not rescuers from Mycenae whom I see ?

sections.label: Section 964
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ τὴν τεκοῦσαν ἥ μʼ ἐγείνατο .
sections.translation: No , but the mother who bore me .

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: καλῶς ἄρʼ ἄρκυν ἐς μέσην πορεύεται —
sections.translation: Then finely she walks to the middle of the net . — And here she comes , splendid in her chariot and dress .

sections.label: Section 967
sections.original: τί δῆτα δρῶμεν ; μητέρʼ ἦ φονεύσομεν ;
sections.translation: What are we going to do ? Shall we kill our mother ?

sections.label: Section 968
sections.original: μῶν σʼ οἶκτος εἷλε , μητρὸς ὡς εἶδες δέμας ;
sections.translation: Surely pity did not seize you , when you saw your mother ?

sections.label: Section 969
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! How can I kill her when she bore me and brought me up ?

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: ὥσπερ πατέρα σὸν ἥδε κἀμὸν ὤλεσεν .
sections.translation: As she killed your father and mine .

sections.label: Section 971
sections.original: ὦ Φοῖβε , πολλήν γʼ ἀμαθίαν ἐθέσπισας —
sections.translation: O Phoebus , you prophesied a great folly —

sections.label: Section 972
sections.original: ὅπου δʼ Ἀπόλλων σκαιὸς ᾖ , τίνες σοφοί ;
sections.translation: Where Apollo is a fool , who are the wise ?

sections.label: Section 973
sections.original: ὅστις μʼ ἔχρησας μητέρʼ , ἣν οὐ χρῆν , κτανεῖν .
sections.translation: You who declared I was to kill my mother , whom it is clearly wrong to kill .

sections.label: Section 974
sections.original: βλάπτῃ δὲ δὴ τί πατρὶ τιμωρῶν σέθεν ;
sections.translation: How can you be hurt by avenging your father ?

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: μητροκτόνος νῦν φεύξομαι , τόθʼ ἁγνὸς ὤν .
sections.translation: I shall stand trial as a matricide , though I was pure before .

sections.label: Section 976
sections.original: καὶ μή γʼ ἀμύνων πατρὶ δυσσεβὴς ἔσῃ .
sections.translation: And by not defending your father , you will be impious .

sections.label: Section 977
sections.original: ἐγὼ δὲ μητρὸς — ; τῷ φόνου δώσω δίκας ;
sections.translation: I , my mother —? To whom will I pay the penalty for her murder ?

sections.label: Section 978
sections.original: τῷ δʼ ἢν πατρῴαν διαμεθῇς τιμωρίαν ;
sections.translation: And to whom , if you give up our father ’ s vengeance ?

sections.label: Section 979
sections.original: ἆρʼ αὔτʼ ἀλάστωρ εἶπʼ ἀπεικασθεὶς θεῷ ;
sections.translation: Was it a fiend who spoke in the likeness of the god ?
